channel.title: NPR Topics: Science
channel.description: The latest health and science news. Updates on medicine, healthy living, nutrition, drugs, diet, and advances in science and technology. Subscribe to the Health & Science podcast.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: This week in science: How much exercise you need, flu resistance and fortified food
item.description: NPR's science podcast Short Wave brings us stories on food fortification, why some people don't seem to get the flu, and a study on how much vigorous exercise you really need.
item.content:encoded: NPR's science podcast Short Wave brings us stories on food fortification, why some people don't seem to get the flu, and a study on how much vigorous exercise you really need.
item.dc:creator: Emily Kwong

item.title: EPA flags microplastics, pharmaceuticals as contaminants in drinking water
item.description: There is public concern about health risks from the chemicals, especially from the Make America Healthy Again movement. The agency's move doesn't in itself guarantee regulation.
item.content:encoded:
There is public concern about health risks from the chemicals, especially from the Make America Healthy Again movement. The agency's move doesn't in itself guarantee regulation.

(Image credit: Justin Sullivan)
item.dc:creator: Will Stone

item.title: Success! After key milestone, Artemis II astronauts speed off toward the moon
item.description: NASA's Artemis II crew has successfully launched on a mission that will take it around the moon and back to Earth. A key maneuver Thursday night sent them hurtling toward their lunar target.
item.content:encoded:
NASA's Artemis II crew has successfully launched on a mission that will take it around the moon and back to Earth. A key maneuver Thursday night sent them hurtling toward their lunar target.

(Image credit: Jim Watson/AFP)
item.dc:creator: Brendan Byrne

item.title: Scientists' efforts to free whale stuck in the Baltic Sea cause online firestorm
item.description: German scientists face a social media firestorm as they try to save a humpback whale in the Baltic Sea.
item.content:encoded: German scientists face a social media firestorm as they try to save a humpback whale in the Baltic Sea.
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz

item.title: Study links falling for corporate buzzwords with poor decision-making
item.description: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with Shane Littrell of Cornell University, whose new study concludes that those who buy into corporate jargon may actually be worse at their jobs.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with Shane Littrell of Cornell University, whose new study concludes that those who buy into corporate jargon may actually be worse at their jobs.
item.dc:creator: Henry Larson

item.title: The oil industry is betting big on plastics. Here's what that means for the future
item.description: Journalist Beth Gardiner says the fossil fuel industry is increasingly reliant upon plastic products. Her book is Plastic Inc.: The Secret History and Shocking Future of Big Oil's Biggest Bet.
item.content:encoded: Journalist Beth Gardiner says the fossil fuel industry is increasingly reliant upon plastic products. Her book is Plastic Inc.: The Secret History and Shocking Future of Big Oil's Biggest Bet.
item.dc:creator: Tonya Mosley

item.title: Liftoff! NASA astronauts have launched on a historic journey to the moon
item.description: NASA's Artemis II mission lifted off from Kennedy Space Center in Florida at 6:35 p.m. Eastern Wednesday. The mission aims to send four astronauts around the moon on a roughly 10-day journey.
item.content:encoded:
NASA's Artemis II mission lifted off from Kennedy Space Center in Florida at 6:35 p.m. Eastern Wednesday. The mission aims to send four astronauts around the moon on a roughly 10-day journey.

(Image credit: Bill Ingalls)
item.dc:creator: Amina Khan

item.title: Domino, the warty frogfish, is the first of its kind to be raised in captivity
item.description: Scientists say the little fish may hold broader lessons for raising other marine species in captivity.
item.content:encoded:
Scientists say the little fish may hold broader lessons for raising other marine species in captivity.

(Image credit: Brenna Hernandez/Shedd Aquarium)
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel

item.title: For years, scientists could only prove that DOGS existed for 10,000 years — until now
item.description: A new study genetically shows dogs existed almost 5,000 years earlier than scientists could prove.
item.content:encoded: A new study genetically shows dogs existed almost 5,000 years earlier than scientists could prove.
item.dc:creator: Henry Larson

item.title: NASA is just days away from historic Artemis II moon launch
item.description: On Wednesday, the crew of NASA's Artemis II could blast off on a mission around the moon and back. No astronaut has ventured out to the moon since the 1970s.
item.content:encoded:
On Wednesday, the crew of NASA's Artemis II could blast off on a mission around the moon and back. No astronaut has ventured out to the moon since the 1970s.

(Image credit: Joe Raedle)
item.dc:creator: Nell Greenfieldboyce
